text:
Okay...

There was a kid really fascinated with owls. That was fine until the owl started following him home. He tried the hood. It slipped. He was quiet for weeks. Then one night he came across an old owl statue outside a bookstore. He leaned against it. As he read a book about owls, a gentle rustle made him tense. That was an owl. Quiet as mice. It looked straight into his eyes. Somehow fascinated, the kid felt enchanted. That's when an idea came.

For Halloween?

He dressed up as an owl. His mom said yes. They carved a tiny owl from an apple. She decorated their tree. They hid the owl. That night...nothing. Maybe luck?

Years passed. Became an artist. That owl...symbolism? Everyone loved it. Awards. Museums. He was famous. That owl wasn’t gone...

One winter storm?

It flew into his living room. Looked at him. Then went back into the corner
